Organization.title: Organization
Organization.description: Represents a single tenant or customer company within the multi-tenant SaaS application. Each organization has isolated data and specific branding/configuration.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Organization entity.

name.description: The official name of the organization.

logoUrl.description: URL to the organization's logo, used for branding in reports and UI.

primaryColor.description: Hexadecimal color code for the organization's primary brand color. (e.g., #4582A1)

backgroundColor.description: Hexadecimal color code for the organization's background brand color. (e.g., #F0F3F4)

accentColor.description: Hexadecimal color code for the organization's accent brand color. (e.g., #70D65C)

globalMarginPercentage.description: A global profit margin percentage applicable to all products for this organization, used in price calculations.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the organization record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the organization record was last updated.

UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores user profile information, roles, and links to the organization they belong to. Assumes external authentication is handled separately.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity (often corresponding to an external authentication system's user ID).

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this user belongs to. Used for multi-tenancy data isolation. (Relationship: Organization 1:N UserProfile)

email.description: The email address of the user.

name.description: The full name of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within their organization (e.g., 'admin', 'seller') or 'superAdmin' for platform-level access.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Factory.title: Factory
Factory.description: Represents a physical or logical factory from which products are sourced, specific to an organization. Corresponds to XLSX sheet tabs.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Factory entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this factory belongs to. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Factory)

name.description: The name of the factory, typically matching an XLSX tab name.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the factory record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the factory record was last updated.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: General information about a product, independent of factory-specific pricing. Prices for a product vary by factory.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this product belongs to. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Product)

name.description: The name of the product, parsed from column A of the XLSX.

unit.description: The unit of measure for the product (e.g., 'KG', 'UN', 'L'), parsed from column B of the XLSX.

description.description: An optional description for the product.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product record was last updated.

ProductFactoryPrice.title: ProductFactoryPrice
ProductFactoryPrice.description: Stores the specific prices for a product when sourced from a particular factory within an organization, as parsed from the XLSX.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProductFactoryPrice entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this price configuration belongs to. (Relationship: Organization 1:N ProductFactoryPrice)

productId.description: Reference to the Product this price applies to. (Relationship: Product 1:N ProductFactoryPrice)

factoryId.description: Reference to the Factory this price is associated with. (Relationship: Factory 1:N ProductFactoryPrice)

closedLoadPrice.description: The price of the product when sold in a full load, specific to this factory, parsed from column C of the XLSX.

fractionalLoadPrice.description: The price of the product when sold in a fractional load, specific to this factory, parsed from column D of the XLSX.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product factory price record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product factory price record was last updated.

Discount.title: Discount
Discount.description: Represents a potential discount that can be applied within an organization, as parsed from the XLSX.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Discount entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this discount belongs to. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Discount)

name.description: A descriptive name for the discount, parsed from column A (second 'Produto' section) of the XLSX.

value.description: The value of the discount. This is a percentage (e.g., 0.10 for 10%), parsed from column C (second 'Produto' section) of the XLSX.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the discount record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the discount record was last updated.

Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a customer of an organization who places orders through the system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this client belongs to. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Client)

name.description: The full name or company name of the client.

email.description: The primary email address of the client.

phone.description: The primary phone number of the client.

address.description: The physical address of the client.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the client record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the client record was last updated.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer order placed by a seller from an organization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this order belongs to. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Order)

clientId.description: Reference to the Client who placed this order. (Relationship: Client 1:N Order)

sellerUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (seller) who created this order. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: The date and time when the order was created or placed.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'draft', 'pending', 'completed', 'cancelled').

totalAmount.description: The final calculated total amount of the order, including all products, prices, and applied discounts.

notes.description: Optional notes or comments about the order.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was last updated.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single line item within an order, detailing a specific product, quantity, and its price at the time of order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product being ordered. (Relationship: Product 1:N OrderItem)

factoryId.description: Reference to the Factory from which this product's price was sourced for this order item. (Relationship: Factory 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product ordered in this line item.

unitPrice.description: The price per unit of the product at the time the order item was placed, reflecting all applicable factory prices, margins, and discounts.

subtotal.description: The calculated subtotal for this line item (quantity * unitPrice).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order item record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order item record was last updated.

definition.description: A collection used to explicitly identify Super Admin users via their user ID. Documents here might be empty or contain minimal data, primarily serving as an existence check for global administrative privileges. Access to this collection would typically be restricted to platform Super Admins and the system itself. This provides authorization independence for the Super Admin role.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who is a Super Admin, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles, including their assigned role ('superAdmin', 'admin', 'seller') and the 'organizationId' they belong to. This collection is crucial for multi-tenancy and role-based access control, as it denormalizes the user's authorization context directly into their profile document. This enables robust Authorization Independence and QAPs for user-specific data and tenant-level access checks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Root collection for each tenant (customer company). Stores global settings, branding (logo, colors), and margin configurations for the organization. Managed by Super Admins and Organization Admins.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique ID of the organization (tenant).

definition.description: Subcollection storing factories specific to an organization. Each document includes the `organizationId` field for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique ID of the organization to which the factory belongs.

params.name: factoryId
params.description: The unique ID of the factory.

definition.description: Subcollection storing general product information for a specific organization. Each document includes the `organizationId` field for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique ID of the organization to which the product belongs.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique ID of the product.

definition.description: Subcollection storing specific pricing for a product from a particular factory within an organization. Each document includes the `organizationId` field for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique ID of the organization.

params.name: productFactoryPriceId
params.description: The unique ID for the product-factory price record.

definition.description: Subcollection storing discount configurations applicable within a specific organization. Each document includes the `organizationId` field for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique ID of the organization.

params.name: discountId
params.description: The unique ID of the discount.

definition.description: Subcollection storing client (customer) details associated with a specific organization. Each document includes the `organizationId` field for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique ID of the organization.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique ID of the client.

definition.description: Subcollection storing customer orders placed within a specific organization. Each document includes the `organizationId` field for authorization independence and QAPs, as well as `sellerUserId` for order ownership.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique ID of the organization.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique ID of the order.

definition.description: Subcollection storing individual line items for a specific order. Each document *must* include the `organizationId` field (denormalized from its parent `Order`) for Authorization Independence and QAP compliance when querying order items directly.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique ID of the organization to which the order item belongs, denormalized for direct queryability and authorization.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the order item.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure is designed to support a multi-tenant SaaS application, prioritizing security, scalability, and debuggability based on the provided core design principles and strategy mandates. The central pillar is multi-tenancy, with all tenant-specific data segregated by an `organizationId`.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
Authorization independence is achieved through extensive denormalization. Every document that belongs to a specific tenant (e.g., Factory, Product, Client, Order) explicitly includes an `organizationId` field. This eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules to determine ownership or belonging. For instance, to check if a user can access a product, the rules will directly compare the `product.organizationId` with the `request.auth.organizationId` (which is obtained from the user's `userProfile` during authentication).

The `UserProfile` documents themselves (`/userProfiles/{userId}`) store the user's `organizationId` and `role`. This means a single `get(/userProfiles/{request.auth.uid})` is sufficient to establish a user's authorization context (their organization and role) at the beginning of a rule evaluation, without further cross-collection reads for tenant-specific data. The `superAdmins` collection provides an independent, existence-based check for platform-level administrators, further isolating this critical authorization context.

Crucially, for nested data like `OrderItems` which are children of `Orders`, the `organizationId` is also denormalized directly into each `OrderItem` document. This means a rule checking `OrderItem` access does not need to perform a `get()` call on the parent `Order` document to determine its `organizationId`, thus maintaining authorization independence even within subcollections.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
The structure ensures Queryability for Authorization Predicates (QAPs) through consistent use of the `organizationId` field. Since every tenant-specific document contains its `organizationId`, `list` operations (queries) can and *must* include `where('organizationId', '==', request.auth.organizationId)` in their client-side queries. Firestore Security Rules can then enforce this predicate by checking `resource.data.organizationId == request.auth.organizationId` or `request.query.resource.data.organizationId == request.auth.organizationId` for `list` operations. This guarantees that clients can only query for data within their own organization, and the rules can efficiently verify this without acting as filters (which is not allowed by Firestore).

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:**
Global platform-level data (`superAdmins`, `organizations`, and `userProfiles` that link to organizations) is kept at the root or dedicated top-level collections. Tenant-specific data is then logically grouped under `organizations/{organizationId}/...` paths (e.g., `factories`, `products`, `clients`, `orders`). This clear segregation ensures that all documents within a given subcollection (e.g., `organizations/{organizationId}/products`) share the same `organizationId` context and thus a homogeneous security posture, greatly simplifying security rules. The `userProfiles` collection stores all user profiles, with `organizationId` and `role` fields, serving as the central hub for user-specific authorization data. The `superAdmins` collection is a dedicated, existence-based collection for global platform administrators.

This design leverages explicit denormalization and structural segregation to create a highly secure, performant, and easily debuggable Firestore structure for a multi-tenant SaaS.
